op_000003.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α] (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x ↔ x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000003.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] (Δ : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) → ∀ {x : α}, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x ↔ x ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)
op_000003.statement_formalization_notes: Used the statement essentially as given, with conservative fully qualified class names to match the exposed namespace structure in Theory.lean.

op_000003.proof_sketch: Use an explicit three-point chain with elements `bot < mid < top`. Define `□bot = bot`, `□mid = top`, `□top = top`, and `⊠bot = top`, `⊠mid = mid`, `⊠top = mid`. All `ACR`, `APS`, and `C5` obligations are discharged by finite case splits. Then take `Δ = [Delta.reft, Delta.box]`, so `Delta.countReft Δ = 1` is odd. For `x = top`, `Delta.act Δ x = □(⊠x) = □mid = top`, hence `x ≐ Delta.act Δ x`. But `⊠top = mid`, so `top ≐ ⊠top` is false. This contradicts the claimed equivalence for all odd-`⊠` words.
op_000003.proof_text:
by
  intro h
  let Δ : Delta := [Delta.reft, Delta.box]
  have hOdd : Odd (Delta.countReft Δ) := by
    decide
  have hFix : ((⊤ : Three) ≐ Delta.act Δ (⊤ : Three)) := by
    simp [Δ, Delta.act, Delta.actSymbol, ACR.Equivalent, threeProv, threeReft, threeLe]
  have hNot : ¬ ((⊤ : Three) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : Three)) := by
    simp [ACR.Equivalent, threeReft, threeLe]
  exact hNot ((h (α := Three) Δ hOdd (x := (⊤ : Three))).1 hFix)
op_000003.counterexample_text: Take `α = Three = {bot, mid, top}` ordered by `bot < mid < top`. Let `□bot = bot`, `□mid = top`, `□top = top`, and `⊠bot = top`, `⊠mid = mid`, `⊠top = mid`. For `Δ = [reft, box]`, the number of `reft` symbols is `1`, so it is odd. At `x = top`, `Delta.act Δ x = □(⊠top) = □mid = top`, so `x ≐ Delta.act Δ x` holds. But `⊠top = mid`, hence `top ≐ ⊠top` is false. Therefore the universal equivalence in the statement is false.

op_000004.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] (Δ : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft Δ) → (∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x) → Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000004.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (Δ : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft Δ) → (∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000004.statement_formalization_notes: Used fully qualified names for the modal classes and HenkinFixpoint to avoid namespace ambiguity; kept the fixed-point relation in notation form.

op_000004.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by instantiating it at `α = Nat` with an `ACR` structure where `⊤ = 0`, `⊥ = 0`, `□n = n + 1`, and `⊠n = 0`. The APS axioms are immediate in this model. For `Δ = []`, `Delta.countReft Δ = 0` is even and `Delta.act Δ = id`, so `0 ≐ Delta.act Δ 0` holds. If the target universal statement were true, we would get `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Nat)`, i.e. some `n` with `n ≐ n + 1`; its second component gives `n + 1 ≤ n`, contradicting `Nat.not_succ_le_self`.
op_000004.prelude_code:
instance : ACR Nat where
  top := 0
  bot := 0
  le := (· ≤ ·)
  le_refl := by intro n; exact Nat.le_refl n
  le_trans := by intro a b c hab hbc; exact Nat.le_trans hab hbc

instance : ACR.Prov Nat where
  prov n := n + 1

instance : ACR.Reft Nat where
  reft _ := 0

instance : ACR.APS Nat where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    exact Nat.succ_le_succ hxy
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    exact Nat.le_refl 0
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    exact Nat.le_refl 0
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hreft
    simpa using hreft
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    show 0 ≤ 0 + 1
    exact Nat.zero_le _
op_000004.proof_text:
by
  intro h
  let Δ : Delta := []
  have hEven : Even (Delta.countReft Δ) := by
    decide
  have hFix : ∃ x : Nat, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x := by
    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
    simp [Δ, Delta.act, ACR.Equivalent]
  have hHenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Nat) := h (α := Nat) Δ hEven hFix
  rcases hHenkin with ⟨⟨n, hn⟩⟩
  have hs : n + 1 ≤ n := by
    simpa [ACR.Prov.prov] using hn.2
  exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n hs
op_000004.counterexample_text: Take `α = Nat` with preorder `≤`, `⊤ = 0`, `⊥ = 0`, `□n = n + 1`, and `⊠n = 0`. Then the APS axioms hold. For `Δ = []`, `Delta.countReft Δ = 0` is even and `Delta.act Δ = id`, so `0 ≐ Delta.act Δ 0`. But `ACR.HenkinFixpoint Nat` would require some `n` such that `n ≐ n + 1`, whose second inequality is `n + 1 ≤ n`, impossible in `Nat`. Thus the stated universal implication is false.

Define `ACR Nat` by reusing the existing `Nat` preorder via `toPreorder := inferInstance` instead of trying to build the preorder fields manually; this avoids the failing `lt_iff_le_not_ge` obligation. Keep `⊤ = 0`, `⊥ = 0`, `□n = n + 1`, and `⊠n = 0`, so the APS axioms remain easy. Then refute the universal statement by taking `Δ = []`: `countReft [] = 0` is even and `0 ≐ Delta.act [] 0` since `Delta.act [] = id`. Any Henkin fixpoint would give some `n` with `n ≐ □n`, hence in particular `n + 1 ≤ n`, contradicting `Nat.not_succ_le_self`.
op_000004.prelude_code:
instance : ACR Nat where
  toTop := ⟨0⟩
  toBot := ⟨0⟩
  toLE := inferInstance
  toPreorder := inferInstance

instance : ACR.Prov Nat where
  prov n := n + 1

instance : ACR.Reft Nat where
  reft _ := 0

instance : ACR.APS Nat where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    exact Nat.succ_le_succ hxy
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    exact Nat.zero_le _
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    exact Nat.le_refl 0
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hreft
    simpa using hreft
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    show 0 ≤ 0 + 1
    exact Nat.zero_le _
op_000004.proof_text:
intro h
let Δ : Delta := []
have hEven : Even (Delta.countReft Δ) := by
  decide
have hFix : ∃ x : Nat, x ≐ Delta.act Δ x := by
  refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
  simp [Δ, Delta.act, ACR.Equivalent]
have hHenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Nat) := h (α := Nat) Δ hEven hFix
rcases hHenkin with ⟨⟨n, hn⟩⟩
have hs : n + 1 ≤ n := by
  simpa [ACR.Prov.prov] using hn.2
exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n hs
op_000004.counterexample_text: Take `α = Nat` with the usual preorder, `⊤ = 0`, `⊥ = 0`, `□n = n + 1`, and `⊠n = 0`. The APS axioms hold in this model: monotonicity of `□` is `Nat.succ_le_succ`, `⊠` is constant so antitone, and the remaining axioms reduce to trivial inequalities involving `0`. Now choose `Δ = []`. Then `Delta.countReft Δ = 0`, which is even, and `Delta.act Δ = id`, so `0 ≐ Delta.act Δ 0`. If the stated universal implication were true, we would obtain `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Nat)`, so some `n : Nat` satisfies `n ≐ □n = n + 1`. Its second inequality says `n + 1 ≤ n`, impossible by `Nat.not_succ_le_self`. Therefore the statement is false.

op_000005.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] (n : ℕ), 0 < n → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ Delta.act (Delta.pow [Delta.box] n) x) ↔ Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000005.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (n : ℕ), 0 < n → ((∃ x : α, ACR.Equivalent x (Delta.act (Delta.pow [Delta.box] n) x)) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000005.statement_formalization_notes: Minimally repaired the previous attempt by fully qualifying the classes and the fixpoint type inside the `ACR` namespace. I also expanded `≐` to `ACR.Equivalent` conservatively to avoid namespace-scoped notation parsing issues in standalone statement validation.

op_000005.proof_sketch: The proposed argument is unsound: from `x ≐ □^(m+1) x`, applying monotonicity `m` times does not yield `□^m x ≐ □^(m+1) x`; it yields `□^m x ≐ □^(2m+1) x`. With only monotonicity of `□`, there is no clear way to extract a Henkin fixpoint from an `n`-periodic point of `□` for arbitrary positive `n`. I do not see a defensible Lean proof from the stated assumptions.

op_000009.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft d) → (Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000009.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Even (Delta.countReft d) → (Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000009.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition using the repository's notation and names.

op_000009.proof_sketch: Instantiate the universally quantified statement at `α = Int` with a concrete `ACR/Prov/Reft/APS` structure: order is the usual `≤`, `⊤ = 0`, `⊥ = 0`, `□x = x + 1`, and `⊠x = 0`. For `d = []`, `Even (Delta.countReft d)` is immediate and the left-hand fixed-point type is inhabited by `0`, since `Delta.act [] = id`. Hence the assumed theorem yields a Henkin fixpoint `x` with `x ≐ x + 1`, so in particular `x + 1 ≤ x`. But `x < x + 1` on `Int`, contradiction.
op_000009.prelude_code:
instance : ACR Int where
  top := 0
  bot := 0
  le := (· ≤ ·)
  le_refl := by intro x; rfl
  le_trans := by intro a b c hab hbc; exact le_trans hab hbc

instance : ACR.Prov Int where
  prov x := x + 1

instance : ACR.Reft Int where
  reft _ := 0

instance : ACR.APS Int where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    simpa using add_le_add_right h 1
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    rfl
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    rfl
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hprov hreft
    simpa using hreft
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    norm_num
op_000009.proof_text:
intro h
have hEven : Even (Delta.countReft ([] : Delta)) := by
  simp [Delta.countReft]
have hiff := h (α := Int) ([] : Delta) hEven
have hfixed : Nonempty {x : Int // x ≐ Delta.act ([] : Delta) x} := by
  refine ⟨⟨0, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor <;> simp [Delta.act, ACR.Equivalent]
have hhenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Int) := hiff.mp hfixed
rcases hhenkin with ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩
have hxle : x + 1 ≤ x := by
  simpa [ACR.HenkinFixpoint, ACR.Equivalent] using hx.2
have hxlt : x < x + 1 := by
  simpa using lt_add_of_pos_right x (show (0 : Int) < 1 by norm_num)
exact (not_le_of_gt hxlt) hxle
op_000009.counterexample_text: Take `α = Int` with its usual preorder, `⊤ := 0`, `⊥ := 0`, `□x := x + 1`, and `⊠x := 0`. The APS axioms hold: `□` is monotone, `⊠` is antitone because it is constant, `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥` is `0 ≤ 0`, `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` is `0 ≤ 1`, and from `x ≤ □y` and `x ≤ ⊠y = 0` we get `x ≤ 0 = ⊠⊤`. For `d = []`, `Even (Delta.countReft d)` holds and `Nonempty {x : Int // x ≐ Delta.act d x}` is witnessed by `x = 0` since `Delta.act [] = id`. If the statement were true, we would get a Henkin fixpoint `x` with `x ≐ □x`, i.e. `x ≐ x + 1`. Its second inequality says `x + 1 ≤ x`, contradicting the strict fact `x < x + 1` in `Int`.

op_000009.proof_sketch: Keep the original counterexample on `Int`, but repair the local instances. Use the existing preorder on `Int` to satisfy the `ACR` superclass fields, set `□x := x + 1` and `⊠x := 0`, and verify the APS axioms by reducing each goal with `change` to a concrete arithmetic inequality. Then instantiate the target statement at `α = Int` and `d = []`. The left side is inhabited by `0` because `Delta.act [] = id`. The resulting Henkin fixpoint gives `x + 1 ≤ x`, contradicting `x < x + 1`.
op_000009.prelude_code:
instance instACRInt : ACR Int where
  top := 0
  bot := 0
  .. (inferInstance : Preorder Int)

instance instProvInt : ACR.Prov Int where
  prov x := x + 1

instance instReftInt : ACR.Reft Int where
  reft _ := 0

instance instAPSInt : ACR.APS Int where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    change x + 1 ≤ y + 1
    simpa using add_le_add_right h 1
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    change (0 : Int) ≤ 0
    exact le_rfl
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    change (0 : Int) ≤ 0
    exact le_rfl
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hprov hreft
    change x ≤ 0
    simpa using hreft
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    change (0 : Int) ≤ 0 + 1
    norm_num
op_000009.proof_text:
intro h
have hEven : Even (Delta.countReft ([] : Delta)) := by
  simp [Delta.countReft]
have hiff :=
  @h Int instACRInt instProvInt instReftInt instAPSInt ([] : Delta) hEven
have hfixed : Nonempty {x : Int // x ≐ Delta.act ([] : Delta) x} := by
  refine ⟨⟨0, ?_⟩⟩
  show 0 ≤ Delta.act ([] : Delta) 0 ∧ Delta.act ([] : Delta) 0 ≤ 0
  simp [Delta.act]
have hhenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Int) := hiff.mp hfixed
rcases hhenkin with ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩
have hxle : x + 1 ≤ x := by
  have hx' := hx.2
  change x + 1 ≤ x at hx'
  exact hx'
have hxlt : x < x + 1 := by
  omega
exact (not_le_of_gt hxlt) hxle
op_000009.counterexample_text: Take `α = Int` with its usual preorder, `⊤ := 0`, `⊥ := 0`, `□x := x + 1`, and `⊠x := 0`. This satisfies `ACR`, `Prov`, `Reft`, and `APS`: monotonicity of `□` is `add_le_add_right`, `⊠` is constant so antitone, `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥` is `0 ≤ 0`, `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` is `0 ≤ 1`, and from `x ≤ ⊠y = 0` we get `x ≤ ⊠⊤ = 0`. Now choose `d = []`. Then `Even (Delta.countReft d)` holds since `countReft [] = 0`, and `Nonempty {x : Int // x ≐ Delta.act d x}` is witnessed by `x = 0` because `Delta.act [] = id`. If the statement were true, we would obtain `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint Int)`, so some `x : Int` satisfies `x ≐ □x = x + 1`. In particular, the reverse inequality gives `x + 1 ≤ x`, contradicting the strict arithmetic fact `x < x + 1`. Therefore the universal statement is false.

op_000009.proof_sketch: Repair the failed `Int` counterexample by switching to `ULift Nat`, which avoids the noncomputable-instance issue and also sidesteps the universe-instantiation problem when specializing the quantified hypothesis. Use the usual preorder on `ULift Nat`, set `⊤ = ⊥ = 0`, define `□x = x + 1` and `⊠x = 0`, and check the APS axioms by reducing them to elementary inequalities on `Nat`. Then instantiate the claimed theorem at `d = []`. The left side is inhabited by `0` because `Delta.act [] = id`. Any Henkin fixpoint would satisfy `n + 1 ≤ n`, contradicting `Nat.not_succ_le_self`.
op_000009.prelude_code:
instance instACRULiftNat : ACR (ULift Nat) where
  toPreorder := inferInstance
  top := ⟨0⟩
  bot := ⟨0⟩

instance instProvULiftNat : ACR.Prov (ULift Nat) where
  prov n := ⟨n.1 + 1⟩

instance instReftULiftNat : ACR.Reft (ULift Nat) where
  reft _ := ⟨0⟩

instance instAPSULiftNat : ACR.APS (ULift Nat) where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    change x.1 + 1 ≤ y.1 + 1
    exact Nat.succ_le_succ h
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    change (0 : Nat) ≤ 0
    exact le_rfl
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    change (0 : Nat) ≤ 0
    exact le_rfl
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hprov hreft
    change x.1 ≤ (0 : Nat) at hreft ⊢
    exact hreft
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    change (0 : Nat) ≤ 0 + 1
    exact Nat.zero_le _
op_000009.proof_text:
intro h
letI : ACR (ULift Nat) := instACRULiftNat
letI : ACR.Prov (ULift Nat) := instProvULiftNat
letI : ACR.Reft (ULift Nat) := instReftULiftNat
letI : ACR.APS (ULift Nat) := instAPSULiftNat
let d : Delta := []
have hEven : Even (Delta.countReft d) := by
  decide
have hiff : Nonempty {x : ULift Nat // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint (ULift Nat)) := by
  simpa using (h (α := ULift Nat) d hEven)
have hfixed : Nonempty {x : ULift Nat // x ≐ Delta.act d x} := by
  refine ⟨⟨⟨0⟩, ?_⟩⟩
  simp [d, Delta.act, ACR.Equivalent]
have hhenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint (ULift Nat)) := hiff.mp hfixed
rcases hhenkin with ⟨⟨n, hn⟩⟩
have hs := hn.2
change n.1 + 1 ≤ n.1 at hs
exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n.1 hs
op_000009.counterexample_text: Take `α = ULift Nat` with the lifted usual preorder, `⊤ := ⟨0⟩`, `⊥ := ⟨0⟩`, `□x := ⟨x.1 + 1⟩`, and `⊠x := ⟨0⟩`. The APS axioms reduce to trivial facts on `Nat`: monotonicity of `□` is `Nat.succ_le_succ`, `⊠` is constant so antitone, `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥` is `0 ≤ 0`, `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` is `0 ≤ 1`, and from `x ≤ ⊠y = 0` we get `x ≤ 0 = ⊠⊤`. Now choose `d = []`. Then `Delta.countReft d = 0`, so the parity assumption holds, and `Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x}` is witnessed by `x = ⟨0⟩` because `Delta.act [] = id`. If the target statement were true, we would obtain a Henkin fixpoint `n` with `n ≐ □n`, hence in particular `n.1 + 1 ≤ n.1`, impossible by `Nat.not_succ_le_self`. Therefore the universal statement is false.

op_000008.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → (Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α))
op_000008.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (d : Delta), Odd (Delta.countReft d) → (Nonempty {x : α // x ≐ Delta.act d x} ↔ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α))
op_000008.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition using the repository’s notation, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000008.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by specializing to a two-point type `CounterBool` with `ff < tt`, `□x := tt`, and `⊠x := not x`. In this model the APS axioms hold by case analysis. For `d = [Delta.reft, Delta.box]`, `Delta.act d` is constantly `tt`, so `{x // x ≐ Delta.act d x}` is inhabited by `tt`. But `ACR.GödelFixpoint CounterBool` is empty, since `x ≐ ⊠x` fails for both `x = ff` and `x = tt`. Therefore the claimed equivalence cannot hold for all `α` and `d`.
op_000008.prelude_code:
inductive CounterBool where
  | ff
  | tt
  deriving DecidableEq, Repr

instance : ACR CounterBool where
  top := CounterBool.tt
  bot := CounterBool.ff
  le x y := x = CounterBool.ff ∨ y = CounterBool.tt
  refl := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp
  trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    rcases hab with ha | hb
    · exact Or.inl ha
    · rcases hbc with hb' | hc
      · cases hb.trans hb'
      · exact Or.inr hc

instance : ACR.Prov CounterBool where
  prov _ := CounterBool.tt

instance : ACR.Reft CounterBool where
  reft
    | CounterBool.ff => CounterBool.tt
    | CounterBool.tt => CounterBool.ff

instance : ACR.APS CounterBool where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    simp [ACR.Prov.prov]
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [ACR.Reft.reft] at h ⊢
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    simp [ACR.Reft.reft]
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hx hy
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [ACR.Prov.prov, ACR.Reft.reft] at hx hy ⊢
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp [ACR.Prov.prov, ACR.Reft.reft]
op_000008.proof_text:
by
  intro h
  let d : Delta := [Delta.reft, Delta.box]
  have hodd : Odd (Delta.countReft d) := by
    native_decide
  have hiff := h (α := CounterBool) d hodd
  have hleft : Nonempty {x : CounterBool // x ≐ Delta.act d x} := by
    refine ⟨⟨CounterBool.tt, ?_⟩⟩
    constructor <;> simp [d, Delta.act, Delta.actSymbol, ACR.Equivalent, ACR.Prov.prov, ACR.Reft.reft]
  have hright : ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint CounterBool) := by
    rintro ⟨⟨g, hg⟩⟩
    cases g <;> simp [ACR.GödelFixpoint, ACR.Equivalent, ACR.Reft.reft] at hg
  exact hright (hiff.mp hleft)
op_000008.counterexample_text: Use the two-point type `CounterBool = {ff, tt}` ordered by `ff ≤ tt`, with `⊤ = tt`, `⊥ = ff`, `□x = tt`, and `⊠x = not x`. Then the APS axioms hold by exhaustive case analysis. For `d = [Delta.reft, Delta.box]`, one has `Delta.act d x = □(⊠x) = tt` for every `x`, so `tt` is a fixed point of `Delta.act d`. However `ACR.GödelFixpoint CounterBool` is empty because `x ≐ ⊠x` fails for both elements. Hence the displayed equivalence is false in this instance.

op_000017.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type*) (a : ACR α) (p : @ACR.Prov α a) (r : @ACR.Reft α a) (aps : @ACR.APS α a p r) (d : Delta), d ≠ [] ∧ Even (Delta.countReft d) ∧ Nonempty {x : α // @ACR.Equivalent α a x (@Delta.act α a p r d x)} ∧ ¬ Nonempty (@ACR.HenkinFixpoint α a p)
op_000017.statement_formalization_notes: Used explicit class witnesses and fully explicit operator applications to avoid ambiguous existential typeclass binders while preserving the original meaning.

  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    change (0 : Nat) ≤ 0 + 1
    exact Nat.zero_le _
op_000017.proof_text:
refine ⟨op000017α, op000017ACR, op000017Prov, op000017Reft, op000017APS, [Delta.reft, Delta.reft], ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· simp
· native_decide
· refine ⟨⟨⟨0⟩, ?_⟩⟩
  change (((⟨0⟩ : op000017α) ≤ ⟨0⟩) ∧ ((⟨0⟩ : op000017α) ≤ ⟨0⟩))
  constructor <;> exact le_rfl
· rintro ⟨⟨n, hn⟩⟩
  have hs := hn.2
  change n.1 + 1 ≤ n.1 at hs
  exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n.1 hs
